[WIP] Improve graphs and change rate function parameters (#4)

* Initial plan

* Add HybridPolynomialRateFunction and HybridLogarithmicRateFunction

Add two new rate function classes to src/rate_functions.py:

- HybridPolynomialRateFunction: constant LR for first tau steps,
  then polynomial decay via (1 + t - tau)^(-r).
- HybridLogarithmicRateFunction: constant LR for first tau steps,
  then logarithmic decay via 1/(1 + r*(t - tau)).

Both ensure continuity at t = tau and follow existing patterns
for validation, docstrings, and structure.

Improve risk score calculation for identity-based risk factors

- Lower digital footprint threshold (2+ platforms vs 10+)
- Add scaled risk weights based on platform count
- Add public profile information exposure risk
- Add username correlation risk detection
- Lower cluster confidence threshold (0.7 vs 0.8)
- Add potential linkage detection for any clusters

These changes ensure risk scores reflect OSINT findings even without
file exposures, which is the primary use case for defense.

feat: redesign editor forms with flat layout and StrataIconPicker

Redesign all four editor views (Agents, Skills, Projects, MCP Servers)
from the old two-column layout with bare TextBox and small-caps labels
to a clean, flat single-column form layout that fits within the
available viewport without overflow.

Changes:

Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

perf: replace O(n) IIFE turn search in Timeline with memoized binary search

The current-turn lookup was an inline IIFE that ran on every render, doing a
linear scan through all turns. During playback currentTime updates at animation
speed (~60fps), so this ran hundreds of times per second on large sessions.

Fix: extract into useMemo keyed on [currentTime, turns]. Since turns are
time-ordered and non-overlapping, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of
linear scan. Falls back to last turn when currentTime is past all turn ranges.

Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:
  _NuGets.Managed.nuspec, _NuGets.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _NuGetsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _NuGetsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _Symbols.Managed.nuspec, _Symbols.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _SymbolsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _SymbolsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Managed/NativeAssets split with dynamic bin-packing

Instead of manually splitting _NuGets into .Managed and .NativeAssets
sub-packages, dynamically bin-pack all nupkgs into ~200 MB numbered
chunks (_NuGets.Dependencies.1, .2, .3, etc.). The parent meta-package
depends on all chunks.

This is self-balancing: as packages grow or shrink, the chunk count
adjusts automatically without any manual curation.

- Delete 8 .Managed/.NativeAssets nuspec files
- Add generic _Dependencies.nuspec template for chunks
- Rewrite nuget-special Cake target with first-fit decreasing bin-packing
- _NativeAssets handling (raw native binaries) is unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: proxy `/api` routes to backend in Vite dev mode (#21)

* fix: add Vite proxy for /api/* routes in dev mode

Vite dev server returned 404 for all /api/* routes (Coach, sessions,
config, apply, streaming) because no proxy was configured to forward
requests to the backend server (server.js on port 4242).

- Add proxy config in vite.config.js forwarding /api/* to port 4242
- Update CLAUDE.md and README.md to document two-server dev workflow

Closes #20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: note port 4242 must be free for Vite proxy to connect

The backend (bin/agentviz.js) auto-selects a free port starting from
4242. If another process holds 4242 the backend binds to 4243+, but
the Vite proxy is hardcoded to 4242 and will fail to connect.

A doc-only approach was chosen over an env-var escape hatch to avoid
introducing new runtime behavior and keep the risk of new bugs at zero.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract DEFAULT_API_PORT to shared config.js

Move the hardcoded port 4242 into a root-level config.js imported by
vite.config.js and bin/agentviz.js. This eliminates the port mismatch
risk where the backend could bind to a different port than Vite proxies
to. Both now read from the same source of truth.

Removes the port-must-be-free doc warnings added in the previous commit
since the shared constant makes them unnecessary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

is already visible in the command listings; the internal wiring is not
needed to run the project.
